items.label: Blue Monster Returns:
items.body: The PGA Tour is back at Trump National Doral’s Blue Monster for the Cadillac Championship, bringing a championship-caliber test to one of golf’s most recognizable venues.

items.label: Signature Event Stakes:
items.body: This is a high-stakes Signature Event with a $20 million purse and no cut, so every round matters and every player is guaranteed four days to score.

items.label: Demanding Setup:
items.body: The Blue Monster is built around water hazards, long sight lines, and a punishing closing stretch, which puts a premium on discipline and ball control.

items.label: Momentum Into May:
items.body: The event sits at an important point in the schedule, giving players one of the last elite tune-ups before the season’s next major stretch.

items.label: Scottie Scheffler (+450 to +550):
items.body: The betting favorite and the most complete player in the event, with elite tee-to-green form that fits a long, penalty-heavy course.

items.label: Cameron Young (+1400 to +1600):
items.body: A powerful, high-upside contender whose driving can create separation if he keeps the ball in play.

items.label: Collin Morikawa (+1600 to +1800):
items.body: One of the best iron players in golf, making him a natural fit for a course that rewards exact approach shots.

items.label: Tommy Fleetwood (+1600 to +2200):
items.body: A polished all-around player whose control and patience suit a difficult no-cut week.

items.label: Russell Henley (+1800 to +2500):
items.body: A steady tee-to-green performer who tends to thrive when accuracy and decision-making are at a premium.

items.label: Justin Thomas (+2200 to +2500):
items.body: A dangerous ceiling player whose creativity and short-game touch can produce a strong week if the putter warms up.

items.label: Hideki Matsuyama (+2500 to +3000):
items.body: A veteran ball striker whose approach game can keep him in contention across four demanding rounds.

items.label: Patrick Cantlay (+2500 to +3500):
items.body: A methodical, high-floor option with the kind of tee-to-green consistency that travels well at difficult venues.

items.label: Sam Burns (+2500 to +3500):
items.body: A strong birdie-maker who can contend if he gains enough with the putter on firm greens.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: Trump National Doral’s Blue Monster Course in Miami, Florida.

items.label: Return to Doral:
items.body: This is a return to a classic championship venue that has been off the PGA Tour schedule for years, giving the week a fresh feel.

items.label: Course Identity:
items.body: The Blue Monster has long been known for water-lined holes, visual pressure off the tee, and a finish that can swing quickly.

items.label: Course:
items.body: Trump National Doral - Blue Monster, Miami, Florida.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: April 30 through May 3, 2026.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: $20 million.

items.label: Format:
items.body: Signature Event, 72 players, no cut.

items.label: Yardage:
items.body: 7,739 yards, par 72.

items.label: Coverage:
items.body: The event is expected to follow the standard PGA Tour Signature Event broadcast package.

items.label: Event Window:
items.body: The tournament runs from April 30 to May 3, 2026.
